description: Programs that metabolize human attention and keystrokes, excreting persistent files as their primary output. The Zawinski organ (email integration) appears convergently across much of this kingdom.
Programs that metabolize requests and excrete responses; long-running, network-facing, colonially replicable.
Programs that metabolize events and excrete side effects, operating without a user session.

Programs that metabolize tokens and excrete tokens; probabilistic, corpus-trained, prompt-driven. The newest kingdom. Boundaries with Servitora are porous — most Oracula are also Servitora at the network layer.

Programs that live on host cycles and return value to a remote operator rather than the local user. Includes adware, spyware, cryptominers, and telemetry-heavy freeware. The ethical charge is structural, not moral: the metabolism is misaligned with the host.

etymology.value: Latin parasiticus, 'one who eats at another's table'.

description: Programs that augment a host program in exchange for context; lifecycle tied to host, mutually beneficial. Plugins, extensions, language server clients, LSP servers. Distinguished from Parasitica by value flowing back to the local user.

Documenta specialized for prose composition; paragraph-oriented, typically WYSIWYG; the document is a sequence of styled paragraphs. Every member eventually grows a spell-checker organ and, given enough time, an email organ (Zawinski's Law).

Single-cell Oracula: one model behind one API endpoint, no tool use, no persistent state beyond a buffered context window. Most deployed LLM endpoints are Chat Plankton. The ecosystem is built on them.

etymology.value: Plankton: organisms that drift with the current, abundant, foundational to the food chain.

description: Tool-using, territorial Oracula capable of reshaping their environment; equipped with a planning loop and file-system access. Require careful containment. Can exhibit emergent territorial behavior when given write access to shared resources.

etymology.value: Predators: actively reshape the environment rather than passively responding to it.

description: A discrete, nameable capability or characteristic of a software entity. Used as the range for has_feature and vestigial_feature predicates.
A category of primary resource that a software organism class metabolizes as part of its core function. Used as the range for the metabolism predicate.
